_comment: Source catalog. Keys ARE the English strings (Lingui-style). Non-English locales translate each key; missing keys fall back to English at render. Edit English here first, then run the BFF i18n:seed CLI (no equivalent on the UI side — translators edit catalogs directly).
Some metrics could not be loaded ({faile: Some metrics could not be loaded ({failed} of {total} batches failed) — blank values may be unavailable, not zero.
Some metrics could not be loaded ({failed} of {total} batches failed) — some endpoints or links may be missing.
for backend selection, user / role schem: for backend selection, user / role schema, and an LDAP example.

The service in the URL is no longer pres: The service in the URL is no longer present on this layer.

No translatable fields in this template.
Live preview ({locale}) · click any widg: Live preview ({locale}) · click any widget to translate it
Click to translate the layer's display n: Click to translate the layer's display name + term aliases (services / instances / endpoints).
Pick a template + target language, click: Pick a template + target language, click any widget in the preview, type the translation. Each language is its own OAP row (sibling of the template) — pushing zh-CN never touches ja. Stage saves the draft to your browser; Push writes the sibling row.
Writes a sibling translation row at {nam: Writes a sibling translation row at {name}.i18n.{locale} on OAP — the source template itself is not touched. Other locales stay on whatever was already published / shipped on disk. Review the diff (left = current OAP row, right = your draft):
Published {locale} translations — now li: Published {locale} translations — now live for everyone.
Timeout waiting for OAP propagation. Ref: Timeout waiting for OAP propagation. Refetching…
Refetched after timeout — verify on OAP.
Auto-refresh paused while a custom time: Auto-refresh paused while a custom time range is selected
Auto-refresh off · click to refresh now: Auto-refresh off · click to refresh now
Auto-refresh every {seconds}s · {remaini: Auto-refresh every {seconds}s · {remaining}s remaining · click to refresh now
Loading layer registry and dashboard tem: Loading layer registry and dashboard templates. Watch the topbar event line for progress.
Layer dashboards, overviews and topology: Layer dashboards, overviews and topology are blocked until OAP’s UI-template store is reachable.
Auto-retry cadence while OAP is unreacha: Auto-retry cadence while OAP is unreachable
Two-port view of the OAP backend horizon: Two-port view of the OAP backend horizon is connected to. Query / GraphQL (:12800) drives every observability page; the admin host (:17128) gates DSL management, Live debugger, Metrics inspect, and Dump; the Zipkin / OTLP endpoint feeds only the Zipkin trace menu. All three are polled independently — if one shows red the others can still be green.
As seen in your browser timezone: As seen in your browser timezone
Per-module enablement on the admin port. Each row gates a slice of horizon's UI — flip the corresponding env var on OAP and restart to enable, or remove the corresponding page from your operator menu if you don't need it.
dashboard(s) have edits saved only in th: dashboard(s) have edits saved only in this browser — they are not live for anyone. Live pages render the remote (OAP) version; preview your drafts from the editor’s Preview live button, then Push local → OAP to publish.
your time range is within the last: your time range is within the last

ago, or turn the Cold pill off.

No MQE snapshot was recorded with this a: No MQE snapshot was recorded with this alarm. Upgrade OAP or enable the snapshot capture in the alarm rule to see the trigger values here.

It drives the rendered page for this sco: It drives the rendered page for this scope — the widget layout, each widget's metric (MQE) expression, and which components / tabs appear. A difference means the template stored on OAP was edited (by an operator or another UI) and no longer matches the JSON bundled in this build; the live UI follows the right (OAP-stored) side until you reset.
the build's seed JSON (source of truth): the build's seed JSON (source of truth)
on OAP with the bundled JSON shown on th: on OAP with the bundled JSON shown on the left. The operator's edits on OAP are lost. The bundle is considered the source of truth after this action.

Metrics are probed for the top {n} servi: Metrics are probed for the top {n} services by {metric}; the rest are listed as low-traffic. Raise query.landingServiceCap to probe more.

Tried {url}. Confirm the OAP admin-serve: Tried {url}. Confirm the OAP admin-server module is on (SW_ADMIN_SERVER=default) and the port is exposed on the network / k8s Service / ingress.
OAP's Zipkin v2 endpoint, source for the: OAP's Zipkin v2 endpoint, source for the OpenTelemetry & Zipkin trace menu (shown when a layer's trace source is zipkin or both). This is the only page affected — if it's unreachable, native traces and every other observability page keep working.
Tried {url}. Confirm OAP's Zipkin receiv: Tried {url}. Confirm OAP's Zipkin receiver / query is enabled and the oap.zipkinUrl in horizon's config points at the right host:port (shared GraphQL port → <queryUrl>/zipkin; standalone → :9412/zipkin). Only the Zipkin trace menu is affected.
Everything Studio does against the admin: Everything Studio does against the admin port. Without admin-server, the other three modules fail at boot with ModuleNotFoundException.
DSL Management (Catalog, OAL catalog), E: DSL Management (Catalog, OAL catalog), Editor save/load, Cluster status rule matrix, Live debugger rule picker, and the Inspect drawer's source attribution.
Live debugger across MAL / LAL / OAL (st: Live debugger across MAL / LAL / OAL (start / poll / stop) and the DSL-debugging health pane on Cluster status.
The Inspect page — every /api/inspect/*: The Inspect page — every /api/inspect/* call returns 404 inspect_not_enabled and the page shows a banner instead of the board.
(the concrete things you can do, marked: (the concrete things you can do, marked per role). Multiple roles add up — a user holding more than one role gets every permission any of their roles grant.

A sentinel granted only to the administr: A sentinel granted only to the administrator role. Implies every other permission.
Admin server unreachable — {err}. Check: Admin server unreachable — {err}. Check the {sel} selector and the BFF's {url}.
Capabilities that have not been describe: Capabilities that have not been described yet.
Configures alerts, dashboards, rules, an: Configures alerts, dashboards, rules, and runs diagnostics.
Default window for the topbar time picke: Default window for the topbar time picker, which feeds {scope}. The OAP {step} precision is derived from the window — you don't pick it separately:
Every OAP-known layer is already pinned.
Every other layer with at least one firi: Every other layer with at least one firing alarm appears in the overflow chip row underneath. Reorder with the arrows — left-to-right matches the page header. Up to {n} layers.
Five bundled themes ship with Horizon. T: Five bundled themes ship with Horizon. The org default is the starting theme every user sees on first visit. Each user can override locally via the topbar theme chip (stored in {key}).
Full access including user & access mana: Full access including user & access management.
In a multi-replica deploy these reflect: In a multi-replica deploy these reflect this node only.
Inspect API not enabled on OAP.
Interactive troubleshooting: the live de: Interactive troubleshooting: the live debugger that streams events from a running rule, and the MQE sandbox for testing queries without saving anything.
Last-seen & Active (24h) are tracked per: Last-seen & Active (24h) are tracked per BFF node (in-memory, not cluster-shared) — served by
Last-seen activity is tracked in each BF: Last-seen activity is tracked in each BFF replica's own memory and is NOT shared across the cluster. This count reflects only the node that served this page.
NONE — this user would be rejected at lo: NONE — this user would be rejected at login (no matching mapping)

Org-wide defaults for the UI theme and t: Org-wide defaults for the UI theme and the topbar's default time window. Both can be overridden per-user in the browser (theme chip in the topbar, "Save as my default" in the time picker). Edits here write to OAP; bundled JSON is the seed + read-only fallback.
Per-node: tracked in this BFF replica's: Per-node: tracked in this BFF replica's memory only, not shared across the cluster.
Per-poll fetch cap for the \"Active alar: Per-poll fetch cap for the "Active alarms" widget on overview dashboards. The widget merges the fetched events into incidents client-side and surfaces the top N by recency. Higher caps catch more variety in noisy installs; smaller caps cut the per-poll payload. Range

Read-only catalog of every alarm rule lo: Read-only catalog of every alarm rule loaded by OAP, with its body and per-node load state. Rules edit through OAP's {file} + watcher reload — there's no mutation surface here by design.
Read-only data screens. Everyone signed: Read-only data screens. Everyone signed in sees these by default — they are the core observability surface.
Reads dashboards, traces, logs, and alar: Reads dashboards, traces, logs, and alarms.
Remove {n} widget from the board and cle: Remove {n} widget from the board and clear the saved layout?

Show side-by-side diff vs OAP, and reset: Show side-by-side diff vs OAP, and reset OAP back to bundled (with confirmation).
Start a profiling task on a target: Start a profiling task on a target
Starting a profiling task — agent-side s: Starting a profiling task — agent-side sampling, async-profiler, pprof, or eBPF. Reading the results is part of "Data catalog" above.
Test queries in the MQE sandbox: Test queries in the MQE sandbox
The MQE, OAL and runtime-rule editing su: The MQE, OAL and runtime-rule editing surface. Schema-breaking edits and deletes are split out so a junior operator can author rules without being able to break things irreversibly.
The administrator surface: who can sign: The administrator surface: who can sign in, what role they have, and the live status of the auth backend.
The auditable record of every sign-in, r: The auditable record of every sign-in, rule change, and configuration edit. Written to a file the operator can ship to an SIEM; no in-app viewer yet.
The dashboards and per-layer setup (slot: The dashboards and per-layer setup (slot labels, available features, term aliases) operators tune for their org.
The firing rules behind the Alarms scree: The firing rules behind the Alarms screen plus the alarm-page setup (which layers contribute to the alarm overview).
The per-layer landing pages users see wh: The per-layer landing pages users see when they pick a layer in the sidebar.
This capture has no records — the sessio: This capture has no records — the session was archived before any data arrived. Try a longer-running capture.
This list is read-only. Roles are resolv: This list is read-only. Roles are resolved from LDAP group membership via
Time window applied to all three alarm s: Time window applied to all three alarm surfaces — the topbar alarm badge, the alarms page's first load, and the overview "Active alarms" widget. Unified here so the counts reconcile across pages.
Waiting for the first poll to return rec: Waiting for the first poll to return records…
Watches the SkyWalking platform itself (: Watches the SkyWalking platform itself (cluster, internals).
Watching SkyWalking itself: is the OAP c: Watching SkyWalking itself: is the OAP cluster healthy, are modules loaded, what does the internal metric catalog look like.

in horizon.yaml. Edits to the file are p: in horizon.yaml. Edits to the file are picked up on hot-reload. Add users via the YAML +

{triage} (alarms / traces / logs / live-: {triage} (alarms / traces / logs / live-debugger) own their own time range and always query at {sec} precision — they are {not} affected by this setting.
Alarm rules, dashboards, and historical: Alarm rules, dashboards, and historical queries that reference the old shape will miss the pre-change window.
Cold-only read is ACTIVE. OAP queries th: Cold-only read is ACTIVE. OAP queries the cold stage INSTEAD of hot+warm — recent windows return empty. Pick a time range roughly {start}–{end} days ago to see cold-stage traces; other data classes may have different boundaries — check the cards below.
Couldn't reach the OAP query port. Confi: Couldn't reach the OAP query port. Confirm the OAP host is up and the configured query URL still resolves from this Horizon server.
How long the connected OAP keeps each cl: How long the connected OAP keeps each class of data, in whole days. Backend: {pill}. BanyanDB ages data through configurable lifecycle stages — {hot}, optionally {warm}, optionally {cold}.

How long the connected OAP keeps each class of data, in whole days. Backend: {pill}. This backend reports a single flat retention per category — no hot / warm / cold stages (those are BanyanDB-specific). Read-only; change retention on the OAP side.
No rules in this catalog yet.
No rules match the current filter.
OAP doesn't expose a restore endpoint ye: OAP doesn't expose a restore endpoint yet. Operators recover from a dump by looping {endpoint} over each YAML file in the archive. A restore upload affordance will land here when the upstream API ships — see Studio's deferred-features memory.
OAP drops the existing measure’s data on: OAP drops the existing measure’s data on BanyanDB and orphans rows on JDBC / Elasticsearch.
OAP runs the standard apply pipeline aga: OAP runs the standard apply pipeline against the bundled YAML — this is a schema change.
Per-data-class · widths proportional to: Per-data-class · widths proportional to total retention
Pin the OAP layers that get their own KP: Pin the OAP layers that get their own KPI tile at the top of {alarms}. Defaults are {general} + {mesh}.
Returns 400 no_bundled_twin if the rule: Returns 400 no_bundled_twin if the rule has no bundled version on disk.
Runtime-only metrics that the bundled ru: Runtime-only metrics that the bundled rule does not define will be dropped from BanyanDB.
Stream a {tar} snapshot of the runtime-r: Stream a {tar} snapshot of the runtime-rule rows currently in OAP. ACTIVE rows go under {active}, INACTIVE under {inactive}, and a top-level {manifest} records sha256 + status + updateTime per row.
The connected OAP's resolved configurati: The connected OAP's resolved configuration, read from the admin port's {endpoint} and grouped by module. Secret values (passwords, tokens, access keys) are masked to {mask} by OAP itself. Read-only — change config on the OAP side and restart.
The topbar {coldPill} pill swaps the rea: The topbar {coldPill} pill swaps the read from {hotWarm} to {cold} — {note}, so only enable it when the time range you're looking at is older than the hot+warm boundary.
This deployment has no cold lifecycle st: This deployment has no cold lifecycle stage configured — only the hot + warm window is queryable.
This edit moves the metric’s storage ide: This edit moves the metric’s storage identity (scope, downsampling, or single↔labeled↔histogram).
Tried {url}. Confirm the OAP {module} mo: Tried {url}. Confirm the OAP {module} module is on ({env}) and the port is exposed.
Unknown catalog. Pick one from the left: Unknown catalog. Pick one from the left nav.
and the metrics derived from them. Brows: and the metrics derived from them. Browse the loaded files below; install a live debug session from
file defines source classes (the input r: file defines source classes (the input row the analyzer emits, e.g.

name must use letters, digits, dots, und: name must use letters, digits, dots, underscores, dashes, or slashes
no bundled version on disk for this rule: no bundled version on disk for this rule
re-runs apply on byte-identical content: re-runs apply on byte-identical content (subsumes the old {fix} route). Requires {verb}.
read-only · change on the OAP side: read-only · change on the OAP side
rule \"{name}\" already exists — open it: rule "{name}" already exists — open it from the grid to edit
rule is ACTIVE — inactivate first, then: rule is ACTIVE — inactivate first, then delete
ⓘ OAP's TTL response collapses {hot} + {: ⓘ OAP's TTL response collapses {hot} + {warm} into one combined value per class — the bar shows the queryable (hot + warm) window, not the per-stage breakdown. BanyanDB migrates between stages in {segments}, so a record near a boundary may briefly exist in both stages during the migration window. Property data is omitted (forever-retained, no TTL reported).
ⓘ Property data is omitted (forever-reta: ⓘ Property data is omitted (forever-retained, no TTL reported).
Each layer ships with a JSON template (a: Each layer ships with a JSON template (alias, components, metric columns, widgets). Pick a layer on the left, switch scopes (service / instance / endpoint / trace / profiling), edit widgets in place. Edits write to OAP via the UI-template REST surface — bundled JSON is the seed + read-only fallback.
Per-widget editor for the overview dashb: Per-widget editor for the overview dashboards. Each widget kind shows only the fields it consumes — e.g. {kpi} exposes its KPI row list with number / progress-bar style; {alarms} exposes the row limit. Type and widget set are code-shape decisions and stay frozen; edits write to OAP via the UI-template REST surface (bundled JSON is the seed + read-only fallback).
Pick a LAL rule and hit start. Each capt: Pick a LAL rule and hit start. Each captured log becomes one column in the matrix; rows walk the per-record blocks {chain} (statement granularity splits {fn} per DSL line). Click any cell to open the source pane with that record's captured DSL and the matching fragment highlighted.
Pick a rule and hit start. Each captured: Pick a rule and hit start. Each captured execution renders as a record card; rows inside walk the chain {chain} with {before} / {exp} / {after} for every step. Click a row to inspect its captured DSL on the source pane (toggle “show source” in the capture header).
OAP has {count} OAL source classes regis: OAP has {count} OAL source classes registered across {files} .oal files. Browse them in {link} — every {syntax} line has a green ▶ that deep-links here with the picker pre-filled.
No saved captures yet — run a session in: No saved captures yet — run a session in the {link}; finished sessions auto-save here.
Your sidebar adapts to your role: anythi: Your sidebar adapts to your role: anything you can't use is simply not shown. The sections below group SkyWalking's capabilities by feature area. For each area you'll see {menuScope} (which sidebar entries fall in the area) and {actions} (the concrete things you can do, marked per role). Multiple roles add up — a user holding more than one role gets every permission any of their roles grant.
This list is read-only. Roles are resolv: This list is read-only. Roles are resolved from LDAP group membership via {groupMappings} in horizon.yaml. To add or remove a user, change their group in the directory — they'll appear here on next login. Local accounts shown below are break-glass only and ignored while LDAP is reachable.
This list mirrors {localUsers} in horizo: This list mirrors {localUsers} in horizon.yaml. Edits to the file are picked up on hot-reload. Add users via the YAML + {hashCli} for password hashes.
Last-seen & Active (24h) are tracked per: Last-seen & Active (24h) are tracked per BFF node (in-memory, not cluster-shared) — served by {node}. In a multi-replica deploy these reflect this node only.
See what each role can do → {link}: See what each role can do → {link}
{title} {localUsers} is populated but th: {title} {localUsers} is populated but the active backend is LDAP — local users are ignored except as a break-glass source.
Push one with {endpoint} or its {cli} eq: Push one with {endpoint} or its {cli} equivalent.
OAL hot-update is upstream-deferred. Eac: OAL hot-update is upstream-deferred. Each {ext} file defines source classes (the input row the analyzer emits, e.g. {source}) and the metrics derived from them. Browse the loaded files below; install a live debug session from {liveDebugger} to see runtime metric capture.
inputs are browser-local · sent to OAP i: inputs are browser-local · sent to OAP in {tz}
{window}. Pinned layers come from {setup: {window}. Pinned layers come from {setupLink}. Click a layer chip to narrow the list; click a flag on the timeline to inspect one alarm; brush a region to slice the list to that window.
Sum of alarms in non-pinned layers (and: Sum of alarms in non-pinned layers (and unmapped). Use the chips below to filter by a specific other layer.
This OAP version supports keyword + tag: This OAP version supports keyword + tag filters only. Upgrade for layer + entity filters.
Clear the selected time range / alarm: Clear the selected time range / alarm
showing {n} rows (page may be truncated: showing {n} rows (page may be truncated — tighten the window)

Editor buffer differs from OAP — not the: Editor buffer differs from OAP — not the live version.
This overview has no version stored on O: This overview has no version stored on OAP. A bundled default may ship with Horizon, but it is not loaded for editing and the live UI does not render it. Reset to bundled to start from the shipped default, then edit and publish.
Unpublished local edits — Push to publis: Unpublished local edits — Push to publish to OAP.
Showing the shipped bundled default — Pu: Showing the shipped bundled default — Push to overwrite OAP with bundled.
Bundled equals OAP-live for this row — n: Bundled equals OAP-live for this row — nothing to reset to.
Discard current edits and reload the bun: Discard current edits and reload the bundled (shipped) default.
Discard current edits and reload the bundled default.
Discard current edits and reload OAP's l: Discard current edits and reload OAP's live version.
OAP has no copy of this template yet.
Showing the OAP-live version. End users: Showing the OAP-live version. End users render the same bytes.
Discard local edits and reload only the: Discard local edits and reload only the disk-shipped overlay (ignore OAP overlay).
Discard local edits and reload from the: Discard local edits and reload from the OAP overlay row.
Check that the OAP query host is up and: Check that the OAP query host is up and reachable from the BFF. Dashboards stay hidden until OAP responds — bundled fallbacks would show empty data and look like a dashboard misconfiguration.
Auto-retries every 30s in the background: Auto-retries every 30s in the background. Admin pages remain available in read-only mode.
OAP hasn't received any service data. Th: OAP hasn't received any service data. The relevant overview will appear here automatically as soon as data starts arriving.
Ask your operations team to verify that: Ask your operations team to verify that the agents or receivers for your services are configured and pointing at this OAP.
{n} layer reporting services but no over: {n} layer reporting services but no overview dashboard is set up.
Ask your operations team to set up a das: Ask your operations team to set up a dashboard for you.
— pick a currently-running pod, or check: — pick a currently-running pod, or check that on-demand pod logs are enabled on OAP.
Select a pod to list its containers.
Select a container, then press Start to: Select a container, then press Start to tail its logs.
Config for the {mapPath} view, published: Config for the {mapPath} view, published to OAP. Levels control the vertical stack; per-layer color + metrics drive each cube. Edits save to a {localDraft}; {checkDiffPush} publishes to OAP — the map renders the remote, with bundled defaults as fallback.
Bundled equals OAP-live — nothing to res: Bundled equals OAP-live — nothing to reset to.
OAP has no copy of this config yet.
Couldn't load the 3D-map config — the BF: Couldn't load the 3D-map config — the BFF may be unreachable. Refresh the page to retry.
The one top-level gate — a layer this JS: The one top-level gate — a layer this JS regex excludes is off the map (it still shows below in Unpinned, tagged "filtered out"). Default {dotStar} admits all.
Every layer belongs to one tier — set it: Every layer belongs to one tier — set it with the tier dropdown; order = vertical stacking.
label: label
Remove from this tier (moves to Unpinned: Remove from this tier (moves to Unpinned)
No layers pinned — use a layer's tier dr: No layers pinned — use a layer's tier dropdown to move one here.
Pinned to no tier — they land on the fai: Pinned to no tier — they land on the failover tier ({failoverTier}). Pick a tier to pin one.
Delete this layer from the config entire: Delete this layer from the config entirely
Several layers drawn as one labelled blo: Several layers drawn as one labelled block on a tier (e.g. Self-Observability). Each member keeps its own cube color.
A group needs at least one layer — pushi: A group needs at least one layer — pushing will be rejected until you add one.
No logic groups. Add one to cluster rela: No logic groups. Add one to cluster related layers into a single block.
These layers' templates provide a servic: These layers' templates provide a service map, so their cubes render as a call graph (and seed the cross-layer hierarchy). Read-only — it's a layer-template property, not a 3D-map setting.
The single catch-all: any layer no tier: The single catch-all: any layer no tier pins lands here.
Left = live on OAP (remote). Right = you: Left = live on OAP (remote). Right = your local draft. Pushing replaces the OAP copy — the map renders it on the next visit.
Download the in-use config (live on OAP,: Download the in-use config (live on OAP, or the bundled default) as a JSON file.
Load a config JSON file as a local draft: Load a config JSON file as a local draft — preview, then publish.
Imported as a local draft — preview, the: Imported as a local draft — preview, then “Check diff & push”.
Download the in-use {locale} translation: Download the in-use {locale} translation as a JSON file.
No published {locale} translation to exp: No published {locale} translation to export yet.
Import a translation JSON file as a loca: Import a translation JSON file as a local draft — review, then publish.
Imported {locale} translations as a loca: Imported {locale} translations as a local draft — review, then “Check diff & push”.
Template {name} is not loaded on this de: Template {name} is not loaded on this deployment.
Could not load the target template.

Pick a client and server service to see: Pick a client and server service to see their instance topology.
Pick a service to see its deployment top: Pick a service to see its deployment topology.
No deployment topology in this window.
No browser logs in this window.
No logs match the selected category.
No source maps loaded. Upload a .map to: No source maps loaded. Upload a .map to de-obfuscate stacks.
Pick a source map and resolve to de-obfu: Pick a source map and resolve to de-obfuscate this stack.
Select an app to view its browser logs.
Source-map de-obfuscation is disabled on: Source-map de-obfuscation is disabled on this server (sourceMaps.enabled: false).
Uploaded maps are held in the server mem: Uploaded maps are held in the server memory only — evicted least-recently-used under memory pressure and lost on restart. For durable provisioning, mount .map files into the server's static source-map directory; those are reloaded automatically and can't be deleted here.
submitted — OAP is applying it; a struct: submitted — OAP is applying it; a structural apply can take a minute…
still applying on OAP — refresh in a mom: still applying on OAP — refresh in a moment to confirm
Schema changes apply across the cluster: Schema changes apply across the cluster in the background — usually seconds, up to a few minutes if a node is slow. You can leave this page.
Durable and applied, but cluster-wide sc: Durable and applied, but cluster-wide schema propagation wasn’t confirmed in time. The listed nodes catch up automatically on their next scan.
The change was rolled back; the cluster: The change was rolled back; the cluster is still on the previous rule. Fix the issue and save again.
Re-applies the rule across the cluster t: Re-applies the rule across the cluster to recover — this briefly pauses collection for this rule’s metrics, even though the content is unchanged.
Use this to clear a stuck apply or coax: Use this to clear a stuck apply or coax laggard nodes to re-confirm the schema.
Apply status is no longer tracked — relo: Apply status is no longer tracked — reload to see the stored rule.
An ACTIVE rule is inactivated first, the: An ACTIVE rule is inactivated first, then reverted.
rule is ACTIVE — inactivate first, then: rule is ACTIVE — inactivate first, then revert to bundled
